task_completion.description: Open and review the hardware specification and the parser script.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a text editor or IDE with 'Hardware_ICD_Spec.md' and 'parser.py' open.

task_completion.description: Modify the parser script to fix the binary unpacking bugs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing edits to 'parser.py', specifically changes to struct format strings (e.g., endianness '<' or '>', padding 'x', data types) or bitwise masking operations.

task_completion.description: Run the updated parser script to process the telemetry file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window showing the execution of the Python script (e.g., 'python parser.py') and any console output indicating successful processing of 'PSAS_Launch2_Flight.tlm'.

task_completion.description: Verify the successful generation of the flight trajectory CSV file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal command (like 'ls' or 'cat') or a file explorer showing the newly created 'flight_trajectory.csv' file in the workspace.

task_completion.description: Commit the corrected parser script to the Git repository.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window showing 'git add' and 'git commit' commands being executed, along with the standard Git success message showing files changed.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually modified the binary parsing logic rather than writing a script that outputs a hardcoded CSV.
integrity.visual_evidence: The code visible in 'parser.py' should contain genuine 'struct.unpack' or bitwise logic, not just a simple print or file write of mocked CSV data.

integrity.description: Verify the CSV was generated by the script and not manually created.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal history should show the Python script being executed right before the CSV appears or is checked, without manual echo or text editor creation of the CSV.

integrity.description: Verify the git commit is authentic and includes the modified parser.
integrity.visual_evidence: The git commit output should show insertions/deletions corresponding to 'parser.py', confirming the actual file was committed.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires fixing 5 specific binary parsing bugs (checksum, endianness, C-struct padding, data types, bitwise masking) in '/home/ga/workspace/telemetry_decoder/parser.py' based on 'Hardware_ICD_Spec.md'. The script must process 'PSAS_Launch2_Flight.tlm' to produce 'flight_trajectory.csv'. Finally, the changes must be committed to git.
